questions.question: Solve for all values of x: |2x - 5| = |x + 3|
questions.explanation: Absolute value equations split into two cases: (2x - 5) = (x + 3) or (2x - 5) = -(x + 3). First gives x = 8; second gives 3x = 2, so x = 2/3. Verify both solutions satisfy the original equation.

questions.question: Find the solution set for |3x - 7| ≤ 2.
questions.explanation: Rewrite as -2 ≤ 3x - 7 ≤ 2. Add 7 to all parts: 5 ≤ 3x ≤ 9. Divide by 3: 5/3 ≤ x ≤ 3.

questions.question: A factory produces widgets with a diameter of 10 cm, with tolerance of at most 0.3 cm. Write an absolute value inequality representing acceptable diameters d, then solve.
questions.explanation: The absolute difference from 10 must be at most 0.3. Rewrite -0.3 ≤ d - 10 ≤ 0.3 and add 10 to get the bounds.

questions.question: Solve |x + 4| - 3 = 5.
questions.explanation: Isolate the absolute value: |x + 4| = 8. This splits into x + 4 = 8 (so x = 4) or x + 4 = -8 (so x = -12).

questions.question: For what values of k does the equation |x - 2| = k have exactly one solution?
questions.explanation: An absolute value equation |x - a| = k has exactly one solution when k = 0, giving the single solution x = a. For k > 0, there are always two solutions.

questions.question: Solve the compound absolute value inequality: |x - 1| > 4 AND |x + 2| < 6.
questions.explanation: |x - 1| > 4 gives x < -3 or x > 5. |x + 2| < 6 gives -8 < x < 4. The intersection is (-8, -3) ∪ (4, 4), which simplifies to -8 < x < -5 or 4 < x < 5 after careful overlap analysis.
